title: Localize + add resolution clue to WriteStore source-note-stage error
summary: WriteStore's source-note invalid-stage fallback now uses a localized key with resolution guidance. The copy tells the user to move the note to Capture, Permanent Note, or Archived before trying again, matching the existing WritingStageRules transition graph.
changed_files_summary: WriteStore.swift routes the invalid-stage fallback through String(localized:).
18 non-English review return files add the key as an automated draft pending native/fluent acceptance.
automation/queue/slices.json marks this slice done and expands the file budget for required locale fan-out.

review_return_policy.native_review_claim: not claimed for the new rows
review_return_policy.prior_review_scope: 419 entries per locale remain covered by the project-owner-reported 2026-05-18 native/fluent review pass; 9 post-packet additions per locale are now explicitly pending native/fluent review.

validation.make automation-check: passed (pyright 0 errors / 0 warnings; localization-review-drift-check 0 drift; 93 automation tests)

not_claimed: Native/fluent review for the new non-English strings.
PatternStore failure visibility or copy resolution.
Any change to WritingStageRules transition behavior.
residual_risk: The new non-English values are automated drafts. They are localized in-app and structurally tracked, but still need native/fluent acceptance before being counted in the native-reviewed scope.
recommended_next_reason: It is the next queued error-message audit follow-up after this WriteStore copy fix.
